response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack software developer with strong web, testing, and some low-latency systems experience, but the role requires deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. The closest match is low-latency DLang services and a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments, which falls far short of principal-level performance architecture for AI hardware.
strengths: Long, consistent software engineering career (15+ years) with a BS in Computer Science
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience whatsoever
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or accelerator optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture at principal hardware level

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU workload experience
Solid general software engineering and performance-adjacent systems work
